article.title: The Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm Now Has Temporal Reasoning
article.preview_text:
In April, we shipped the token-efficient memory algorithm and mentioned that multi-session reasoning and temporal reasoning were the hardest categories in memory.
Today, we’re shipping meaningful

tweets.text:
Try the updated algorithm today on Mem0.

Same API. No migration needed.

Temporal Reasoning is on by default for new projects. Memory Decay can be enabled from the dashboard or SDK.

Read more:

> Updated Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm with Temporal Reasoning- https://t.co/kYMZ3v7boy
> Get a free API key: https://t.co/FY8G4bOyVE
> Temporal Reasoning: https://t.co/TRZbs1G5hG
> Memory Decay: https://t.co/PYm2ZHdL2b

profile_bio.description: Memory Layer for your AI agents. Open source: https://t.co/HqLHhUMUpN

value.string_value: Mem0’s updated token-efficient memory algorithm adds Temporal Reasoning and Memory Decay, reaching 92.5% on LoCoMo and 94.4% on LongMemEval while staying under a <7,000-token retrieval budget.
The Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm Now Has Temporal Reasoning

tweets.text:
What changed: 

The April algorithm shipped with single-pass extraction and hierarchical retrieval: extract memories efficiently, retrieve across multiple signals, and stay far below full-context token costs.

The updated algorithm has: 
1. Temporal Reasoning-  gives every memory a time signature: when the event happened, whether it is ongoing or completed, how precise the timing is, and what kind of memory it is.

2. Memory Decay- adds recency-aware ranking, so stale memories stay available without dominating current queries.

Together, they make retrieval more time-aware: what changed, what is still true, and what should no longer dominate the result set.

article.title: The Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm Now Has Temporal Reasoning
article.preview_text:
In April, we shipped the token-efficient memory algorithm and mentioned that multi-session reasoning and temporal reasoning were the hardest categories in memory.
Today, we’re shipping meaningful

tweets.text:
Try the updated algorithm today on Mem0.

Same API. No migration needed.

Temporal Reasoning is on by default for new projects. Memory Decay can be enabled from the dashboard or SDK.

Read more:

> Updated Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm with Temporal Reasoning- https://t.co/kYMZ3v7boy
> Get a free API key: https://t.co/FY8G4bOyVE
> Temporal Reasoning: https://t.co/TRZbs1G5hG
> Memory Decay: https://t.co/PYm2ZHdL2b

profile_bio.description: Memory Layer for your AI agents. Open source: https://t.co/HqLHhUMUpN

value.string_value: Mem0’s updated token-efficient memory algorithm adds Temporal Reasoning and Memory Decay, reaching 92.5% on LoCoMo and 94.4% on LongMemEval while staying under a <7,000-token retrieval budget.
The Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm Now Has Temporal Reasoning

tweets.text:
What changed: 

The April algorithm shipped with single-pass extraction and hierarchical retrieval: extract memories efficiently, retrieve across multiple signals, and stay far below full-context token costs.

The updated algorithm has: 
1. Temporal Reasoning-  gives every memory a time signature: when the event happened, whether it is ongoing or completed, how precise the timing is, and what kind of memory it is.

2. Memory Decay- adds recency-aware ranking, so stale memories stay available without dominating current queries.

Together, they make retrieval more time-aware: what changed, what is still true, and what should no longer dominate the result set.

article.title: The Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm Now Has Temporal Reasoning
article.preview_text:
In April, we shipped the token-efficient memory algorithm and mentioned that multi-session reasoning and temporal reasoning were the hardest categories in memory.
Today, we’re shipping meaningful

tweets.text:
Try the updated algorithm today on Mem0.

Same API. No migration needed.

Temporal Reasoning is on by default for new projects. Memory Decay can be enabled from the dashboard or SDK.

Read more:

> Updated Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm with Temporal Reasoning- https://t.co/kYMZ3v7boy
> Get a free API key: https://t.co/FY8G4bOyVE
> Temporal Reasoning: https://t.co/TRZbs1G5hG
> Memory Decay: https://t.co/PYm2ZHdL2b

profile_bio.description: Memory Layer for your AI agents. Open source: https://t.co/HqLHhUMUpN

value.string_value: Mem0’s updated token-efficient memory algorithm adds Temporal Reasoning and Memory Decay, reaching 92.5% on LoCoMo and 94.4% on LongMemEval while staying under a <7,000-token retrieval budget.
The Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm Now Has Temporal Reasoning

tweets.text:
What changed: 

The April algorithm shipped with single-pass extraction and hierarchical retrieval: extract memories efficiently, retrieve across multiple signals, and stay far below full-context token costs.

The updated algorithm has: 
1. Temporal Reasoning-  gives every memory a time signature: when the event happened, whether it is ongoing or completed, how precise the timing is, and what kind of memory it is.

2. Memory Decay- adds recency-aware ranking, so stale memories stay available without dominating current queries.

Together, they make retrieval more time-aware: what changed, what is still true, and what should no longer dominate the result set.

article.preview_text:

Today, we’re shipping meaningful

tweets.text:
Try the updated algorithm today on Mem0.

profile_bio.description: Memory Layer for your AI agents. Open source: https://t.co/HqLHhUMUpN

value.string_value: Mem0’s updated token-efficient memory algorithm adds Temporal Reasoning and Memory Decay, reaching 92.5% on LoCoMo and 94.4% on LongMemEval while staying under a <7,000-token retrieval budget.
The Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm Now Has Temporal Reasoning

tweets.text:
What changed: 

The April algorithm shipped with single-pass extraction and hierarchical retrieval: extract memories efficiently, retrieve across multiple signals, and stay far below full-context token costs.

The updated algorithm has: 
1. Temporal Reasoning-  gives every memory a time signature: when the event happened, whether it is ongoing or completed, how precise the timing is, and what kind of memory it is.

2. Memory Decay- adds recency-aware ranking, so stale memories stay available without dominating current queries.

Together, they make retrieval more time-aware: what changed, what is still true, and what should no longer dominate the result set.

tweets.text:

2. Memory Decay- adds recency-aware ranking, so stale memories stay available without dominating current queries.

Together, they make retrieval more time-aware: what changed, what is still true, and what should no longer dominate the result set.

article.title: The Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm Now Has Temporal Reasoning
article.preview_text:
In April, we shipped the token-efficient memory algorithm and mentioned that multi-session reasoning and temporal reasoning were the hardest categories in memory.
Today, we’re shipping meaningful

tweets.text:
Try the updated algorithm today on Mem0.

Same API. No migration needed.

Temporal Reasoning is on by default for new projects. Memory Decay can be enabled from the dashboard or SDK.

Read more:

> Updated Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm with Temporal Reasoning- https://t.co/kYMZ3v7boy
> Get a free API key: https://t.co/FY8G4bOyVE
> Temporal Reasoning: https://t.co/TRZbs1G5hG
> Memory Decay: https://t.co/PYm2ZHdL2b

profile_bio.description: Memory Layer for your AI agents. Open source: https://t.co/HqLHhUMUpN

value.string_value: Mem0’s updated token-efficient memory algorithm adds Temporal Reasoning and Memory Decay, reaching 92.5% on LoCoMo and 94.4% on LongMemEval while staying under a <7,000-token retrieval budget.
The Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm Now Has Temporal Reasoning

tweets.text:
What changed: 

The April algorithm shipped with single-pass extraction and hierarchical retrieval: extract memories efficiently, retrieve across multiple signals, and stay far below full-context token costs.

The updated algorithm has: 
1. Temporal Reasoning-  gives every memory a time signature: when the event happened, whether it is ongoing or completed, how precise the timing is, and what kind of memory it is.

2. Memory Decay- adds recency-aware ranking, so stale memories stay available without dominating current queries.

Together, they make retrieval more time-aware: what changed, what is still true, and what should no longer dominate the result set.

article.title: The Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm Now Has Temporal Reasoning
article.preview_text:
In April, we shipped the token-efficient memory algorithm and mentioned that multi-session reasoning and temporal reasoning were the hardest categories in memory.
Today, we’re shipping meaningful

tweets.text:
Try the updated algorithm today on Mem0.

Same API. No migration needed.

Temporal Reasoning is on by default for new projects. Memory Decay can be enabled from the dashboard or SDK.

Read more:

> Updated Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm with Temporal Reasoning- https://t.co/kYMZ3v7boy
> Get a free API key: https://t.co/FY8G4bOyVE
> Temporal Reasoning: https://t.co/TRZbs1G5hG
> Memory Decay: https://t.co/PYm2ZHdL2b

profile_bio.description: Memory Layer for your AI agents. Open source: https://t.co/HqLHhUMUpN

value.string_value: Mem0’s updated token-efficient memory algorithm adds Temporal Reasoning and Memory Decay, reaching 92.5% on LoCoMo and 94.4% on LongMemEval while staying under a <7,000-token retrieval budget.
The Token-Efficient Memory Algorithm Now Has Temporal Reasoning

tweets.text:
What changed: 

The April algorithm shipped with single-pass extraction and hierarchical retrieval: extract memories efficiently, retrieve across multiple signals, and stay far below full-context token costs.

The updated algorithm has: 
1. Temporal Reasoning-  gives every memory a time signature: when the event happened, whether it is ongoing or completed, how precise the timing is, and what kind of memory it is.

2. Memory Decay- adds recency-aware ranking, so stale memories stay available without dominating current queries.

Together, they make retrieval more time-aware: what changed, what is still true, and what should no longer dominate the result set.

tweets.text:
What changed: 

The April algorithm shipped with single-pass extraction and hierarchical retrieval: extract memories efficiently, retrieve across multiple signals, and stay far below full-context token costs.

The updated algorithm has: 
1. Temporal Reasoning-  gives every memory a time signature: when the event happened, whether it is ongoing or completed, how precise the timing is, and what kind of memory it is.

2. Memory Decay- adds recency-aware ranking, so stale memories stay available without dominating current queries.

Together, they make retrieval more time-aware: what changed, what is still true, and what should no longer dominate the result set.
